ucs.title: [LLM] Leafminer (Raspite) credential harvesting via LaZagne + Mimikatz cascade after watering-hole landing
ucs.description: Hunts Leafminer's signature credential-access chain on Middle East government/energy hosts: a browser-spawned dropper (from a Symantec-documented watering-hole compromise) followed by execution of LaZagne/Mimikatz-class tools dumping LSASS, SAM, NTDS, and browser/Vault password stores within a short window. This combination is characteristic of Leafminer (aka Raspite, Symantec/CrowdStrike clusters) which Symantec specifically observed using LaZagne and a custom .NET credential-harvester, an unusual public+custom toolset pairing for Iranian-aligned activity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","python*.exe","*.exe") AND (Processes.process IN ("*lazagne*","*LaZagne*","*mimikatz*","*sekurlsa*","*lsadump*","*procdump*lsass*","*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*","*reg* save*sam*","*reg* save*system*","*reg* save*security*","*ntdsutil*","*vaultcmd*","*all browsers*")) ) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","iexplore.exe","firefox.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename count as browser_spawn_count] | where browser_spawn_count >= 1 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name process_name process browser_spawn_count
ucs.kql: let watering_hole_seed = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","iexplore.exe","firefox.exe") | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") | project SeedTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, BrowserChild=FileName, BrowserParent=InitiatingProcessFileName; let cred_dump = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lazagne","LaZagne","all browsers","all_browsers")) or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("sekurlsa","lsadump","mimikatz","privilege::debug")) or (FileName =~ "procdump.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass") or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has_cs "MiniDump") or (ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"reg(\.exe)?\s+save\s+.*hklm\\(sam|system|security)") or (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ifm") or (FileName =~ "vaultcmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/listcreds") | project DumpTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName; cred_dump | join kind=inner watering_hole_seed on DeviceId | where DumpTime between (SeedTime .. SeedTime + 24h) | extend MinutesAfterBrowserChild = datetime_diff('minute', DumpTime, SeedTime) | project DumpTime, SeedTime, MinutesAfterBrowserChild, DeviceName, AccountName, BrowserParent, BrowserChild, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName | sort by DumpTime desc
ucs.rationale: Symantec's 2018 Leafminer report explicitly documented this group's reliance on (a) compromised legitimate websites of Middle East entities as drive-by delivery, and (b) the LaZagne credential dumper paired with Mimikatz-style LSASS access. Most Iranian APTs (APT34/OilRig, APT35/Charming Kitten, MuddyWater) use spearphishing, not watering-holes — so a browser-spawned interpreter chained with LaZag

ucs.title: [LLM] Leafminer SMB password-spraying preceded by internal network service scanning
ucs.description: Detects Leafminer's documented intranet recon-and-spray pattern: a single internal host enumerates SMB/445 across the subnet (T1046), then within minutes pivots to authenticating against many internal accounts over SMB with low per-account attempt counts (T1110.003 password spraying). Symantec attributed exactly this scan-then-spray sequence — using THC-Hydra and a custom SMB brute-forcer — to Leafminer operations against Saudi/Lebanese targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true dc(All_Traffic.dest) as smb_targets count as smb_attempts from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=445 AND All_Traffic.src_category="internal" AND All_Traffic.dest_category="internal" by All_Traffic.src _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | where smb_targets >= 25 | rename src as scan_src | join type=inner scan_src [ | tstats summariesonly=true dc(Authentication.user) as sprayed_users dc(Authentication.dest) as auth_dests count as auth_attempts from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action="failure" AND Authentication.app IN ("win:remote","smb","ntlm","kerberos") by Authentication.src _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | rename src as scan_src | where sprayed_users >= 10 AND (auth_attempts / sprayed_users) <= 5 ] | eval scan_to_spray_window="<=10min" | table _time scan_src smb_targets smb_attempts sprayed_users auth_dests auth_attempts scan_to_spray_window | sort - _time
ucs.kql: let scanners = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(1d) | where RemotePort == 445 and ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt","ConnectionFailed") | where ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP) and ipv4_is_private(LocalIP) | summarize SmbTargets = dcount(RemoteIP), SmbAttempts = count(), ScanStart=min(Timestamp), ScanEnd=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 10m) | where SmbTargets >= 25; let sprayers = IdentityLogonEvents | where Timestamp > ago(1d) | where Protocol in ("NTLM","Kerberos") and ActionType == "LogonFailed" | summarize SprayedUsers = dcount(AccountName), AuthFailures = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(DeviceName), SprayStart=min(Timestamp), SprayEnd=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 10m) | where SprayedUsers >= 10 and (toreal(AuthFailures) / toreal(SprayedUsers)) <= 5.0; scanners | join kind=inner sprayers on DeviceName | where SprayStart between (ScanStart .. ScanEnd + 30m) | project DeviceName, ScanStart, SmbTargets, SmbAttempts, SprayStart, SprayedUsers, AuthFailures, DistinctTargets | sort by ScanStart desc
ucs.rationale: Leafminer's tradecraft is unusual among Iranian APTs in being heavily intranet-noisy: Symantec recovered staging-server logs showing the operators ran port scans for SMB and then immediately ran password-spraying tools (THC-Hydra, custom SMB brute-forcer) from the same beachhead — without OPSEC throttling. Generic spray detections fire on external-origin Azure AD activity; this rule is specificall
